events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Wine Tasting & Cheese Train
events.description: Step aboard for a relaxed and thoughtfully curated Wine Tasting Experience on the train, where scenic rail travel meets a guided exploration of classic wines from around the world - sponsored by Vine Lore. Guests will enjoy a five-course wine tasting, each pour selected to showcase a range of styles and regions. The experience is designed to unfold at an easy, enjoyable pace, encouraging conversation, discovery, and time to savor each wine. Each table will be presented with a generous shared cheese board, available throughout the journey for guests to enjoy alongside the wines at their leisure. You'll ride in one of our beautiful vintage lounge cars. Tickets are sold either by the half table for 2 people (may be shared with 2 other guests), a full table for 4, and a limited number of single seats. Wines on the menu: Decoy Brut Cuvee Kumusha Sauvignon Blanc Cest La Vie Rouge (Pinot Noir & Syrah Blend) Larchago Tempranillo - Rioja, Spain Graham's Six Grapes Ruby Port
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Road to the Million Championship Night
events.description: The Old West Futurity is set to return to Heber Valley June 20, bringing some of the nation’s top horse trainers, competitors, owners, and equine athletes to the Wasatch County Event Complex for elite competition and western tradition. The competition culminates with the Road to the Million Championship Night on Saturday, June 20, featuring elite Heading, Heeling, and Breakaway competitors competing for more than $2.5 million in prizes, including the chance to win a $1 million bonus. The evening will also include live performances by western entertainers Dave Stamey and Gunnar Latham. Date: Saturday, June 20, 2026 Location: Wasatch County Event Complex, Heber City Gates Open: 6:00 PM Opening Ceremonies: 7:00 PM Tickets: $35 General Admission | Children 5 & Under Free For tickets and additional event information, visit: https://www.oldwest.com/pages/heber-finals-and-concert
events.location: Wasatch County Events Complex, 415 Southfield Road Heber City, Utah 84032

events.title: Rock-n-Roll Train
events.description: The Rock-n-Roll Train is a 90 minute round trip ride on the historic Heber Train featuring great rock-n-roll music, trivia, and train history hosted by Mark Nelson, Executive Director. A great date night! You'll be singing along to some of the greatest rock hits ever. This year we will have a 21-and-older car with beer and wine available. You are not allowed to bring alcohol on the train! And, when the train returns you can stick around for a live concert featuring The Flashback Brothers. There will be food and drink and lots of good times at the Depot after the train! The onboard Train Bar will be open during the concert. Alcohol must be consumed on the train.

events.title: Music Elevated State Tour
events.description: We’re bringing a symphonic celebration of America’s 250th birthday to Utah’s Little Switzerland! Enjoy a performance by our musicians against green backdrops and crisp alpine air, right in the heart of Midway’s Town Square. The program has a patriotic flair, featuring favorite classical works, Broadway selections, American folk music, and more, including: - Works from celebrated American composers like Aaron Copland and Leroy Anderson, including John Williams’ Liberty Fanfare. Western-inspired pieces \(perfect for iconic Western vistas\)—Elie Siegmeister’s Western Suite and Aaron Copland’s “Hoe-Down” from Rodeo. Western Suite was first recorded with the Utah Symphony under Maurice Abravanel in 1971. - Iconic classical works like the beautiful Adagietto from Mahler’s Symphony No. 5, a Maurice Abravanel favorite and first recorded by the Utah Symphony in 1975. - Performances by violinist Aubree Oliverson, a Utah native and recent Masterworks soloist for the Utah Symphony. - Soprano Amy Owens \(former Utah Opera Resident Artist\) performs “Glitter and Be Gay” from Bernstein’s Candide, as well as “Quando m’en vo” from Puccini’s La bohème and Ricky Ian Gordon’s “Simple Child” from The Grapes of Wrath. These pieces honor Utah’s world-class arts legacy: La bohème was Utah Opera’s first production in 1978 and The Grapes of Wrath was co-commissioned and premiered in 2007...
events.location: Midway Music on the Square, Midway, UT

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Rigby Summer
events.description: Formerly touring as summerland, Oklahoma-based songwriter, Rigby Summer, weaves an Americana soundscape that reflects the road she’s traveled, from her Kansas City jazz upbringing to the beach pop of her former home in southern California to the red dirt roots of her current home in Stillwater, Oklahoma. While her influences range from Mary Chapin Carpenter to The Frames, Sia Furler to Johnny Cash, her sound often draws comparisons to Ingrid Michaelson and Peggy Lee—a little modern, a little old-fashioned, always earnest. In 2017, after a five-year hiatus from performing music, she founded the Monday@Modella songwriter pop-up and the ALL MUSIC STARTS LOCAL initiative and started sharpening her musical teeth, slowly carving a niche for herself in the Oklahoma scene. Since then, she has had the privilege of sharing the stage with and learning from a myriad of local and touring artists, including Bill Scorzari, Belle Plaine, Blake Berglund, Heather Styka and many more. She was also given honorable mention in the 2017 Woody Guthrie Festival Songwriting Contest and was a semi-finalist in the 2019 Wyoming Singer-Songwriter Contest, in which she was voted as the audience choice winner for her round. Hher first full-length album “Geography” was released in October 21 and broke the top ten on the NACC Folk Chart in November 2021....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.description: Ride the historic TBM Avenger \#309 when we join our friends at the CAF Utah Wing for their annual Planes and Horsepower Show. TBM 309 is the only aircraft on Colorado's register of historic places. It flew over Queen Elizabeth's Coronation and appeared in Close Encounters of the Third Kind. Book a flight with her today at the ticket link in this event!

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Longevity, Simplified: An Expert-Led Conversation
events.description: Black Rock Mountain Resort’s Onyx Club is partnering with local wellness experts for its June Speaker Series event, *Longevity, Simplified: What Actually Moves the Needle for Your Health*, to focus on the practical application of long-term health optimization. The enlightening event, which is free and open to the community, will take place on Wednesday, June 24, from 5:30 to 7:00 p.m. at Black Rock Mountain Resort in Heber City, Utah. The event brings together two respected, local leaders in longevity, health, and performance: Dr. Timothy Duerler of Ovation Private Health and Connor Darnbrough, co-founder of The Smart Fit Method. Designed to cut through the noise surrounding wellness trends and longevity advice, the discussion will focus on the four pillars of Longevity — sleep, exercise, nutrition, and stress management — and how individuals can apply evidence-based strategies to improve daily performance, vitality, and long-term health outcomes. Participants are encouraged to attend with questions for the Q&A to follow the brief, informative presentations. Attendees will gain insight into: The lifestyle factors that have the greatest impact on longevity and overall health How advanced diagnostics such as lab testing, body composition analysis, and VO₂ max assessments can personalize wellness strategies Which health metrics matter most for optimizing performance and extending health span Practical ways to implement sustainable habits within demanding, real-world schedules Darnbrough will share the story behind The Smart Fit Method, a science-driven training system developed through his own experience overcoming severe injuries and rebuilding strength after a professional skiing career. The approach, embraced by everyone from elite athletes looking to boost their performance to seniors prioritizing their healthspan, emphasizes efficient, sustainable training designed to improve resilience, recovery, and long-term physical performance. events.title: Thrive | FREE Youth Well-Being Skills Summer Program
events.description: Thrive is a FREE evidence-based program equipping Summit & Wasatch County students in grades 6-12 with vital life skills. Imagine our youth… Confidently managing stress amidst school pressures and social uncertainties. Nurturing positive relationships built on empathy and effective communication. Making healthy choices for their physical and mental well-being. Thrive empowers them with these skills and more! Students meet for one hour weekly on Zoom, with a cohort of up to ten peers and a Thrive facilitator, to learn, practice, and reflect on valuable well-being skills. Registration closes on 7/1 at 11:59 PM...
